item.text: I’m closing the remaining validator gaps in two places: actual runtime emission of micro-task packet/summary artifacts, and an end-to-end API test for `GET /role_mailbox/index` that exercises both valid and invalid structured exports. I’ll inspect the current micro-task registry path and existing mailbox API tests first, then patch only the in-scope files and rerun the short-target verification plus `just gov-check`.

item.text: I’ve confirmed the micro-task path currently stops at metadata backfill plus in-memory validation. I’m wiring actual `packet.json` / `summary.json` writes into that same runtime path, then I’ll add direct tests for success and failure on the written artifacts and an API-level mailbox index test under the existing role-mailbox test surface.

item.text: I’ve scoped the product change: a micro-task emission wrapper around `RegisterMts`, `StartMt`, `BindSession`, `UnbindSession`, `RecordIteration`, and `CompleteMt`, plus a loader/emitter that writes canonical `packet.json` and `summary.json`. After that I’ll add the direct artifact tests and the end-to-end mailbox index API tests.

The micro-task emitter is in place. I found one path-normalization detail while wiring it: the metadata summary path needs the runtime display path, not an absolute filesystem path. I’m correcting that before I move on to tests.

I’m moving to the mailbox endpoint now. I’m tightening `GET /role_mailbox/index` so invalid structured exports return an actual HTTP error instead of a successful string response, then I’ll add end-to-end tests for both the valid and invalid cases.

The code and test surfaces are patched. I’m formatting the four touched files now, then I’ll run the short-target packet test suite and `just gov-check` so we can see the exact remaining signal, if any.

item.text: The full micro-task suite surfaced a regression in the existing validation-failure tests: the invalid-register cases are no longer returning machine-readable validation JSON. I’m tracing that before I run the mailbox suite, because it means my emission wrapper changed failure behavior on the `register_mts` path.

The regression is test-surface, not product logic: those three existing register-validation tests were still running without a runtime workspace env, so they were failing before they ever reached schema validation. I’m fixing those tests to use the same temp workspace/env guard as the other structured-runtime cases, then I’ll rerun the full packet suites.

item.text: The register-validation payload is still there; the job framework stores it as `Terminal error: <json>`. I’m teaching the packet test helper to strip that transport prefix so the tests keep validating the actual machine-readable payload instead of failing on the wrapper text.

item.text: The mailbox API tests compile-failed on test scaffolding, not product code. I passed the wrong trait object into `AppState` and used `InMemoryLlmClient`, which is gated out for this integration target. I’m replacing that with a tiny local `LlmClient` stub and wiring `DuckDbFlightRecorder` through both the recorder and diagnostics slots correctly.

item.text:
Implemented the remaining validator items without widening scope.

Micro-task runtime emission is now real, not metadata-only. The register path emits disk artifacts, and the mutating micro-task ops now refresh them through the same wrapper in [workflows.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Structured-Collaboration-Schema-Registry-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/workflows.rs#L2927), [workflows.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Structured-Collaboration-Schema-Registry-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/workflows.rs#L3055), and [workflows.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Structured-Collaboration-Schema-Registry-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/workflows.rs#L3502). That writes and validates `.handshake/gov/micro_tasks/{wp_id}/{mt_id}/packet.json` and `summary.json` for `RegisterMts`, `StartMt`, `BindSession`, `UnbindSession`, `RecordIteration`, and `CompleteMt`.

The mailbox API now exercises read-time validation end to end. `GET /role_mailbox/index` returns an actual HTTP 500 on invalid structured exports instead of a successful string response in [role_mailbox.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Structured-Collaboration-Schema-Registry-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/role_mailbox.rs#L54). I added direct artifact tests for micro-task success and failure in [micro_task_executor_tests.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Structured-Collaboration-Schema-Registry-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/tests/micro_task_executor_tests.rs#L1129) and [micro_task_executor_tests.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Structured-Collaboration-Schema-Registry-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/tests/micro_task_executor_tests.rs#L1225), plus API-level valid/invalid mailbox index tests in [role_mailbox_tests.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Structured-Collaboration-Schema-Registry-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/tests/role_mailbox_tests.rs#L541) and [role_mailbox_tests.rs](/D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Structured-Collaboration-Schema-Registry-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/tests/role_mailbox_tests.rs#L576). No Loom-protected file was touched.

- `rustfmt --edition 2021 src/backend/handshake_core/src/workflows.rs src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/role_mailbox.rs src/backend/handshake_core/tests/micro_task_executor_tests.rs src/backend/handshake_core/tests/role_mailbox_tests.rs`

- `just gov-check`
